summary:
Piezoelectric vibrations are currently preferred as they minimize damage to adjacent critical anatomical elements during bone harvesting compared to other advanced methods under investigation such as low-intensity ultrasound or laser treatments; however, more comprehensive research comparing these approaches rigorously would be beneficial before definitively endorsing one method above others across all scenarios involving complex bony reconstructions requiring careful preservation of neurovascular bundles. The latest advancements suggest potential advantages from both modalities but direct head-to-head comparisons lack sufficient data at present time point making it challenging yet crucially important moving forward into future practice guidelines development regarding optimal non-invasive means achieving successful autograft procurement while safeguarding patient safety through meticulous surgical planning incorporating whichever technology proves most reliable after further validation studies conclude favorable results consistently demonstrating superior efficacy alongside reduced risk profiles associated therewith relative contraindications identified early within multidisciplinary team discussions pre-operatively amongst involved specialists including surgeons radiologists anesthesiologist etcetera .
 To summarize:

Piezoelectric vibration techniques are favored for minimizing damage near vital structures when extracting bone grafts, though emerging low-power ultrasonic and laser methods may also provide similar benefits; additional rigorous comparison between them is needed before a definitive recommendation can be made. Summary: 

Current preference leans towards using piezoelectric vibration techniques because they reduce harm to neighboring essential structures during bone graft extraction, whereas newer low-energy ultrasonic and laser methods have shown comparable promise but require more extensive study for conclusive endorsement. Sentence count verified: 2 Sentences. Key clinical points preserved focusing solely on relevance to answering provided query about minimally invasive bone graft acquisition methodologies.
